UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and preferences within the HomIllu application, linked to Firebase Authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's selected language preference ('en' for English, 'te' for Telugu). Defaults to 'en' upon signup.

householdIds.description: An array of IDs referencing Household entities that this user is a member of. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Household)

Household.title: Household
Household.description: Represents a family or group that shares tasks, shopping lists, calendar events, and a family tree.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Household entity.

name.description: The name of the household (e.g., 'Smith Family', 'My Home').

description.description: An optional description for the household.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the household was created.

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created and owns this household. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Household)

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a task or chore assigned within a household.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

householdId.description: Reference to the Household this task belongs to. (Relationship: Household 1:N Task)

title.description: The title or brief description of the task.

description.description: A detailed description of the task (optional).

assignedToId.description: Reference to the UserProfile assigned to this task (optional). (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Task)

dueDate.description: The due date and time for the task (optional).

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the task has been completed.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the task was marked as completed (optional, only if isCompleted is true).

recurrence.description: Describes the recurrence pattern of the task (e.g., 'none', 'daily', 'weekly', 'monthly').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the task was created.

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this task. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Task)

ShoppingListItem.title: ShoppingListItem
ShoppingListItem.description: Represents an item on a shared shopping list for a household.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ShoppingListItem entity.

householdId.description: Reference to the Household this shopping item belongs to. (Relationship: Household 1:N ShoppingListItem)

name.description: The name of the shopping item (e.g., 'Milk', 'Eggs').

quantity.description: The quantity of the item needed.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the quantity (e.g., 'pcs', 'kg', 'liter', optional).

dueDate.description: The date by which the item should be purchased (optional).

isChecked.description: Indicates whether the item has been purchased or checked off the list.

isFavorite.description: Indicates whether this item is a favorite for quick re-addition.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the shopping item was added.

addedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who added this item. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ShoppingListItem)

CalendarEvent.title: CalendarEvent
CalendarEvent.description: Represents a family event or appointment on a shared calendar.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CalendarEvent entity.

householdId.description: Reference to the Household this event belongs to. (Relationship: Household 1:N CalendarEvent)

description.description: A detailed description of the event (optional).

startDateTime.description: The start date and time of the event.

endDateTime.description: The end date and time of the event (optional, for events with duration).

isAllDay.description: Indicates if the event is an all-day event.

location.description: The physical or virtual location of the event (optional).

invitedMemberIds.description: An array of IDs referencing UserProfiles invited to this event (optional). (Relationship: UserProfile N:N CalendarEvent)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the event was created.

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N CalendarEvent)

Person.title: Person
Person.description: Represents an individual family member within the interactive family tree.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Person entity.

householdId.description: Reference to the Household this person belongs to. (Relationship: Household 1:N Person)

name.description: The full name of the family member.

email.description: The email address of the family member for consent and collaboration.

isConfirmed.description: Whether the family member has confirmed their participation in the digital family hub.

photoUrl.description: URL to the family member's profile photo (optional).

birthDate.description: The birth date of the family member (optional).

deathDate.description: The death date of the family member (optional).

gender.description: The gender of the family member (e.g., 'male', 'female', 'other', optional).

description.description: A descriptive text about the family member, possibly AI-generated (optional).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this person was added to the family tree.

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who added this person. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Person)

Relationship.title: Relationship
Relationship.description: Represents a relationship between two Person entities in the family tree.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Relationship entity.

householdId.description: Reference to the Household this relationship belongs to. (Relationship: Household 1:N Relationship)

person1Id.description: Reference to the first Person in the relationship. (Relationship: Person 1:N Relationship)

person2Id.description: Reference to the second Person in the relationship. (Relationship: Person 1:N Relationship)

startDate.description: The start date of the relationship (e.g., marriage date for spouses, optional).

endDate.description: The end date of the relationship (e.g., divorce date, optional).

description.description: A descriptive text about the relationship, possibly AI-generated (optional).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this relationship was added.

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who added this relationship. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Relationship)

definition.description: Stores user profiles, preferences, and an array of household IDs the user belongs to. The `userId` matches the Firebase Authentication UID. This collection is private to each user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Represents a shared family or group. Includes an `ownerId` and a denormalized `members` map (`members: { [uid: string]: "role" }`) to define user access roles (e.g., 'member', 'admin') within the household for authorization.

params.name: householdId
params.description: The unique identifier for the household.

definition.description: Stores tasks and chores specific to a household. Includes denormalized `householdMembers` map (copied from the parent household) for authorization independence, allowing atomic security rule evaluation.

params.name: householdId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent household.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier for the task.

definition.description: Stores shopping list items for a specific household. Includes denormalized `householdMembers` map (copied from the parent household) for authorization independence.

params.name: householdId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent household.

params.name: shoppingListItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the shopping list item.

definition.description: Stores calendar events specific to a household. Includes denormalized `householdMembers` map (copied from the parent household) for authorization independence.

params.name: householdId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent household.

params.name: calendarEventId
params.description: The unique identifier for the calendar event.

definition.description: Stores individual family members as nodes in the family tree for a specific household. Includes denormalized `householdMembers` map (copied from the parent household) for authorization independence.

params.name: householdId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent household.

params.name: personId
params.description: The unique identifier for the person in the family tree.

definition.description: Stores relationships between persons in the family tree for a specific household. Includes denormalized `householdMembers` map (copied from the parent household) for authorization independence.

params.name: householdId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent household.

params.name: relationshipId
params.description: The unique identifier for the relationship.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for the HomIllu application prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence via Denormalization and Structural Segregation.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) via Denormalization:**

1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored in a top-level collection, with `userId` directly corresponding to `request.auth.uid` from Firebase Authentication. This inherently provides robust, path-based authorization where users can only access their own profile. The `householdIds` array within `UserProfile` allows users to quickly identify which households they are members of without complex queries or rule dependencies.

2.  **Household Membership Denormalization:** The `Household` entity (`/households/{householdId}`) is the central collaborative unit. To ensure Authorization Independence for all child entities, each `Household` document will include a `members` map (e.g., `members: { [uid: string]: "member" | "admin" }`). This map lists all user IDs that are part of the household along with their roles.

3.  **Subcollection Denormalization:** For all subcollections of `Household` (i.e., `tasks`, `shoppingListItems`, `calendarEvents`, `persons`, `relationships`), their security depends on the membership of their parent `Household`. To eliminate costly and transaction-breaking `get()` calls in security rules, each document within these subcollections will contain a denormalized copy of the parent `Household`'s `members` map, named `householdMembers` (e.g., `householdMembers: { [uid: string]: "member" | "admin" }`). This means security rules can evaluate access solely based on the `resource.data` of the document being accessed, achieving atomic operations and simplified, highly performant rules like `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid in resource.data.householdMembers;`. A Cloud Function will be responsible for propagating changes from a `Household`'s `members` map to all relevant child documents upon update.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) Support:**

1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** Read access is secured by matching `userId` with `request.auth.uid`. No global list operation is required or permitted, inherently satisfying QAP.

2.  **Households (`/households/{householdId}`):** To list households a user belongs to, clients can either query the `households` collection with `where("members.[request.auth.uid]", "exists", true)` (or a similar query that leverages specific membership roles, potentially with a helper array `memberUids` if direct map key queries are insufficient for `list` rules) or, more practically, iterate through the `householdIds` in the user's `UserProfile` and perform individual `get()` requests. The security rule `allow read: if request.auth.uid in resource.data.members;` ensures that only members can read a specific household document. This prevents broad, unfiltered `list` queries, aligning with QAP.

3.  **Subcollections (`/households/{householdId}/tasks`, etc.):** `List` operations for these subcollections are always performed within the context of a specific `householdId` (e.g., `/households/household123/tasks`). The security rule `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid in resource.data.householdMembers;` effectively ensures that only authorized members of that specific household can `list` its tasks, shopping items, etc. This structure naturally enforces QAP as the query path itself provides crucial authorization context, and the document-level denormalized data validates the user's access within that context.

**Structural Segregation:**
All documents within a given collection or subcollection (`tasks`, `shoppingListItems`, `persons`, etc.) share the exact same security requirements – access by `householdMembers`. There are no mixed-security-posture documents (e.g., public vs. private) within the same collection, simplifying security rules significantly.
